title: Taxes and Social Security: The Money That Supports Society
description: Why do we pay taxes? How does social security work? Let's learn together!

scenes.speech: Understanding taxes helps you see how society works! 🏛️
scenes.title: Taxes and Social Security

scenes.speech: Why do we pay taxes in the first place?
scenes.title: The Purpose of Taxes
scenes.content: Taxes fund public services like schools, hospitals, roads, police, and fire departments. By sharing the cost, we create a richer society.
scenes.highlight: Japan's tax revenue: approximately ¥75 trillion/year (2024) → about ¥600,000 per person

scenes.speech: What kinds of taxes are there? Let's take a look!
scenes.title: Types of Taxes
scenes.content: Direct taxes: income tax, corporate tax, inheritance tax. Indirect taxes: consumption tax (10% on purchases), liquor tax, tobacco tax. There are about 50 types, including property tax and automobile tax.
scenes.highlight: 1% of consumption tax = about ¥2.5 trillion in tax revenue. 10% = ¥25 trillion! A representative tax paid by everyone

scenes.speech: Is the 'progressive tax' on income tax fair?
scenes.title: The Progressive Tax System
scenes.content: The higher your income, the higher your tax rate. 'Progressive tax': 5% for income under ¥1.95 million, 20% for ¥330,000 - ¥695,000, 45% for over ¥40 million. This system reduces inequality by having high-income earners pay more.
scenes.highlight: Income tax rate: 5% for under ¥1.95 million → 45% for over ¥40 million + 10% local tax = up to 55%

scenes.speech: What do social insurance premiums pay for?
scenes.title: Types of Social Insurance
scenes.content: Social insurance includes: ① health insurance (illness, injury), ② pension insurance (old age, disability, survivor's benefits), ③ employment insurance (unemployment), and ④ workers' compensation insurance (workplace accidents). It's like an invisible tax deducted automatically from your salary.
scenes.highlight: Social insurance premium burden: about 30% of salary for office workers (split between employer and employee, with employee paying around 15%)

scenes.speech: Will I receive pension benefits in the future?
scenes.title: The Pension System
scenes.content: Japan's pension system is a support system where working-age people support retirees. With fewer working-age people and more retirees, it's essential to build your own retirement assets (iDeCo, NISA)!
scenes.highlight: 2023: 3 working-age people support 1 retiree → 2060: 1.5 working-age people support 1 retiree

scenes.speech: Is tax saving sneaky or smart?
scenes.title: Legitimate Tax Saving
scenes.content: Legally reducing taxes is called 'tax saving' or 'tax planning.' Examples include: furusato nōzei (hometown tax), NISA, iDeCo, medical expense deductions, and mortgage loan deductions. Knowing these can make a big difference!
scenes.highlight: Furusato nōzei: donate to your favorite municipality → receive gifts and reduce taxes!

scenes.speech: Let's find taxes in our daily lives!
scenes.title: Taxes in Everyday Life
scenes.content: Check your convenience store receipts! You should see consumption tax listed. Food at supermarkets is 8%, while eating out and alcohol are 10%. Your electricity and water bills also have tax information. You're interacting with taxes every day!
scenes.highlight: Food (reduced tax rate) 8% vs eating out and general goods 10%. Did you notice the tax rate difference?

scenes.speech: It's citizens' duty to monitor tax waste!
scenes.title: Taxes and Democracy
scenes.content: Taxes are everyone's money. Politicians decide how to use it, but citizens choose them through elections! You can vote once you turn 18. 'No taxation without representation' – this is the relationship between democracy and money.
scenes.highlight: 'No taxation without representation': a phrase that sparked the American Revolutionary War

scenes.speech: Now you understand how society works! Next up: funding 🎉
scenes.title: Lesson Complete!
